name: Gandi LiveDNS API
what_it_does: REST API for managing Gandi-registered domains, LiveDNS records, SSL certificates, email forwarding, and organization billing from European domain registrar focused on privacy and ethics.
use_cases: Automating DNS record management for European-hosted domains
Programmatically registering and renewing domains via Gandi's extensive TLD catalog
Managing email forwarding and mailbox configuration for domain-based email
Querying domain WHOIS and expiry status for portfolio management agents
Issuing and renewing Let's Encrypt certificates via the certificate API
not_for: Bulk low-cost registrations (Gandi is priced for quality/ethics, not volume discounts)
US-centric TLD portfolios where Namecheap/Porkbun are cheaper
best_when: An agent manages domains for privacy-conscious users or European customers who prefer ethical hosting providers.
avoid_when: You need the cheapest possible domain pricing or complex DNS routing rules.

auth.notes: API keys, Personal Access Tokens (PATs) with scopes, and OAuth2 for third-party apps. LiveDNS uses separate authentication. PATs support domain-level and operation-level scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; pay per domain/certificate
pricing.paid_tiers: .com ~$17-20/year; LiveDNS premium zones from €2/month; pricing above budget registrars
pricing.notes: Gandi positions on ethics and support quality over price. Prices are higher than Porkbun/Namecheap but includes better support and privacy defaults.

performance.rate_limits: 60 requests/minute for most endpoints; documented in API headers

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec available which improves agent usability. Error responses are JSON with structured codes. Rate limit headers returned on responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: LiveDNS record operations support PUT for upsert semantics. Domain registration is naturally idempotent once registered.
known_agent_gotchas: LiveDNS API endpoint is separate from the main Gandi API (api.gandi.net vs livedns.gandi.net)
Rate limit is 60 req/min - agents doing bulk DNS updates must throttle
Domain availability checks are best-effort; actual registration may still fail due to race conditions
Some ccTLDs have special eligibility requirements that the API won't surface until registration attempt

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR-compliant, EU-hosted. PAT system allows scoped access. OAuth2 for third-party app delegation. Better scope model than Porkbun. WHOIS privacy included by default on most TLDs.
